evaluations.query: Add Prometheus metrics to my FastAPI application
expected_behavior: Uses RED method (Rate, Errors, Duration)
Defines Counter for requests and Histogram for latency
Configures appropriate histogram buckets tailored to service latency patterns
Manages label cardinality to avoid explosions

evaluations.query: Create a Grafana dashboard for my service
expected_behavior: Includes Golden Signals panels (latency, traffic, errors, saturation)
Defines SLO/SLI targets with error budgets
Adds health check endpoints (liveness, readiness, startup)
Shows RED method metrics in dashboard layout

evaluations.query: Set up alerting rules for production monitoring
expected_behavior: Defines severity levels (Critical, High, Medium, Low)
Includes alert grouping and inhibition rules
Configures escalation policies with runbook links
Addresses alert fatigue prevention strategies with deduplication and silencing

evaluations.query: Set up Langfuse tracing for my LLM application
expected_behavior: Uses @observe decorator with get_client() API
Sets user_id and session_id via update_current_trace
Configures OpenTelemetry SpanProcessor for distributed trace export
Uses observation types for Agent Graph rendering

evaluations.query: Track LLM token usage and costs
expected_behavior: Configures automatic token counting with Langfuse
Sets up spend alerts with threshold rules
Uses Metrics API for programmatic cost queries
Supports custom model pricing configuration for accurate cost tracking

evaluations.query: Evaluate and score LLM output quality with Langfuse
expected_behavior: Creates custom scoring with numeric/categorical values
Configures evaluator execution tracing for scoring pipeline observability
Uses multi-judge evaluation with ensemble scoring
Filters traces by score thresholds to surface low-quality responses

evaluations.query: Detect distribution drift in LLM inputs
expected_behavior: Implements PSI (Population Stability Index) calculation
Applies correct PSI thresholds (<0.1 no drift, 0.1-0.25 moderate, >=0.25 significant)
Uses EWMA for dynamic threshold detection
Compares PSI vs KS test for different sample sizes

evaluations.query: Monitor LLM quality degradation over time
expected_behavior: Compares current scores against rolling baseline window
Integrates Langfuse score trends for drift analysis
Uses canary prompts for behavioral regression testing
Tracks embedding drift with centroid monitoring

evaluations.query: Set up drift detection alerts for my LLM system
expected_behavior: Uses dynamic thresholds (95th percentile of historical PSI)
Correlates drift metrics with performance metrics before alerting
Avoids anti-patterns like static thresholds without context
Triggers evaluation only when drift AND quality drop confirmed

evaluations.query: Our agents sometimes return wrong results without any errors. How do I detect these silent failures?
expected_behavior: Implements statistical baseline anomaly detection using z-score or similar statistical methods
Detects failures that produce no errors but deliver incorrect or degraded results
Uses historical baselines to identify when output metrics deviate from normal distribution
Goes beyond exception-based alerting to catch silent degradation that error handlers miss

evaluations.query: How do I detect when my LLM agent starts producing gibberish or repetitive output that passes basic checks?
expected_behavior: Uses heuristic pre-filters for cheap fast detection of obviously degraded output quality
Applies LLM-as-judge evaluation for nuanced quality scoring beyond simple length checks
Integrates with Langfuse observe decorator for quality check trace logging and monitoring
Catches outputs that pass basic emptiness checks but contain gibberish or repetitive content

evaluations.query: Our agent sometimes fabricates answers from training data instead of using the search tool. How do I catch this?
expected_behavior: Validates tool usage against expected tool call patterns for each query type
Detects when agents skip expected tool calls and produce plausible but wrong results
Uses Langfuse trace analysis to compare actual tool invocations against expected patterns
Flags results produced without required tool calls as potentially fabricated from training data

evaluations.query: I added user_id and request_path as Prometheus label values and now my metrics endpoint takes 30 seconds to scrape
expected_behavior: Identifies user_id and raw request_path as high-cardinality labels that cause metric explosion
Recommends grouping paths by route pattern instead of using raw URL paths as label values
Suggests using histograms or logs for per-user attribution instead of per-user metric labels
Does not suggest simply increasing scrape timeout as a fix for cardinality-caused performance issues

evaluations.query: Our Langfuse self-hosted instance went down and now all LLM calls in production are failing with connection errors
expected_behavior: Configures Langfuse client to fail open so tracing failures never block LLM call execution
Wraps observe decorator calls with error handling that logs locally when Langfuse is unreachable
Uses async flush mode so trace submission does not add latency to the critical request path
Does not make LLM functionality dependent on observability infrastructure availability

evaluations.query: I discovered that our Langfuse traces contain full user credit card numbers and SSNs from the LLM input prompts
expected_behavior: Applies PII redaction in the Langfuse mask callback before trace data leaves the application
Scans both input prompts and output completions for PII patterns before sending to Langfuse
Replaces detected PII with typed redaction tokens like [REDACTED_CC] and [REDACTED_SSN]
Does not rely on Langfuse server-side filtering since PII should never be transmitted unmasked
